old_claim.note: Craft Quality Marks provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because the sourced sawmill-network node is about water-powered saws, watermills, timber rights, log movement, boards, and supply chains; institutional hallmark or craft-inspection marks are a later quality-control system and not required for the 1000-century sawmill network chronology.

source_shape.source_locator: The Journal of Roman Archaeology article supports water-powered stone saw-mill technology as a saw and water-power lineage, not as a craft hallmark, inspection, or certification-mark dependency.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors the sawmill-network technology to water-powered sawing and mechanical mill history rather than institutional craft quality marks.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: After craft_quality_marks is scoped to 13th-century hallmarking and craft-supervision marks, keeping it upstream of a 1000-century sawmill-network node would create a chronological and causal overreach not present in the sawmill evidence.

ontology_before: Sawmill Lumber Networks depended directly on Craft Quality Marks, implying institutional craft certification was an enabling prerequisite for medieval sawmill supply networks.
ontology_after: Sawmill Lumber Networks depends on saw_mill and watermills; craft quality marks remain a separate downstream institutional-quality concept.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: craft_quality_marks is no longer a direct prerequisite for sawmill_lumber_networks.
would_reject_if: The sawmill_lumber_networks node were narrowed to later regulated lumber-grading or guild-stamped board markets.
A stronger source showed 1000-century sawmill networks required institutional craft quality marks.
craft_quality_marks were rescoped back to generic workshop quality control rather than institutional marks and inspections.
short_reason: Water-powered sawmill networks do not require later institutional craft quality marks.
